posts.name: p. raghib
posts.cooked:
if I recall correctly, he changed for 750ml format first, then a couple years later he changed the mags as well.

Anyone recall which vintage?

thanks very much

-paul

posts.name: Alex Goldstein
posts.cooked:
I have 2009 Ponsot in 750, mag, and double mag that all use the synthetic cork.

I read that Ponsot started using these in 2008 vintage, but I haven’t had any '08 Ponsot so I don’t know if this is true.

posts.name: A. So
posts.cooked: 2008 is under the plastic cork (at least the CV is). I can’t speak to the magnums though.

posts.name: Ch@rlie F|_|
posts.cooked:
A. So:

2008 is under the plastic cork (at least the CV is). I can’t speak to the magnums though.

if i had a magnum under the plastic cork I’d be so sad.

posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked: Had an '08 Morey under plastic last year. It was drinking very well.

posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked: got rid of all my plastic closed Ponsot.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Yes, 2007 was the last vintage under natural cork chez Ponsot, and 2008 the first vintage under the “Ardea Seal”.
